parts.text:
That's a great request. A build for both serious gaming like Cyberpunk 2077 and resource-intensive AI training requires a strong focus on both the CPU (for AI workload processing) and an NVIDIA GPU (for CUDA acceleration, which is standard for AI).

I have found three optimized configurations under your **$1400** budget.

***

### 1. Optimal Hybrid Build (Recommended)

This configuration prioritizes the **NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070** for excellent 1440p gaming performance and robust CUDA support crucial for AI training tasks. The **Intel i7-13700K** and **32GB of DDR5 RAM** provide a strong foundation for both purposes.

| Component | Part Name | Price (USD) | Purchase Link |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| **CPU** | **Intel Core i7-13700K** | $360 | [link](https://example.com/intel-i7-13700k) |
| **GPU** | **NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070** | $549 | [link](https://example.com/rtx-4070) |
| Motherboard | ASUS Prime Z790-P WiFi | $189 | [link](https://example.com/asus-prime-z790-p) |
| RAM | Corsair Vengeance 32GB DDR5 6000MHz | $115 | [link](https://example.com/corsair-vengeance-32gb-ddr5) |
| Storage | Kingston A400 240GB SATA SSD | $25 | [link](https://example.com/kingston-a400-240gb) |
| PSU | EVGA 600 W1 600W | $49 | [link](https://example.com/evga-600w) |
| Case | NZXT H5 Flow | $89 | [link](https://example.com/nzxt-h5-flow) |
| Cooler | Stock Cooler (Included) | $0 | [link](https://example.com/stock-cooler) |
| **Total Cost** | | **$1376** | |

**Justification:**
This build is highly optimized for your budget. The i7-13700K has a high core count and high clock speeds for fast compilation and training times. The RTX 4070 is a mid-to-high-end GPU that supports the CUDA platform, giving you a major advantage in AI model development. The 32GB of fast DDR5 RAM is ideal for handling the large datasets associated with AI work and heavy multitasking.

***

### 2. Alternative Hybrid Build

This configuration is almost identical to the first, swapping out the case for a slightly different model with excellent airflow.

| Component | Part Name | Price (USD) | Purchase Link |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| **CPU** | **Intel Core i7-13700K** | $360 | [link](https://example.com/intel-i7-13700k) |
| **GPU** | **NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070** | $549 | [link](https://example.com/rtx-4070) |
| Motherboard | ASUS Prime Z790-P WiFi | $189 | [link](https://example.com/asus-prime-z790-p) |
| RAM | Corsair Vengeance 32GB DDR5 6000MHz | $115 | [link](https://example.com/corsair-vengeance-32gb-ddr5) |
| Storage | Kingston A400 240GB SATA SSD | $25 | [link](https://example.com/kingston-a400-240gb) |
| PSU | EVGA 600 W1 600W | $49 | [link](https://example.com/evga-600w) |
| Case | Corsair 4000D Airflow | $94 | [link](https://example.com/corsair-4000d) |
| Cooler | Stock Cooler (Included) | $0 | [link](https://example.com/stock-cooler) |
| **Total Cost** | | **$1381** | |

***

### 3. AMD GPU Focused Build (More Gaming, Less AI Focus)

This configuration saves money by swapping the NVIDIA GPU for an AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT, which performs similarly in gaming but is generally less efficient for AI training due to the software ecosystem.

| Component | Part Name | Price (USD) | Purchase Link |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---|
| **CPU** | **Intel Core i7-13700K** | $360 | [link](https://example.com/intel-i7-13700k) |
| **GPU** | **AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT** | $499 | [link](https://example.com/rx-7800xt) |
| Motherboard | ASUS Prime Z790-P WiFi | $189 | [link](https://example.com/asus-prime-z790-p) |
| RAM | Corsair Vengeance 32GB DDR5 6000MHz | $115 | [link](https://example.com/corsair-vengeance-32gb-ddr5) |
| Storage | Kingston A400 240GB SATA SSD | $25 | [link](https://example.com/kingston-a400-240gb) |
| PSU | EVGA 600 W1 600W | $49 | [link](https://example.com/evga-600w) |
| Case | NZXT H5 Flow | $89 | [link](https://example.com/nzxt-h5-flow) |
| Cooler | Stock Cooler (Included) | $0 | [link](https://example.com/stock-cooler) |
| **Total Cost** | | **$1326** | |

***

### Important Consideration: Cooling and Storage

Please note a critical point on the component list provided by the optimizer:

*   **Cooling:** The **Intel Core i7-13700K** is a high-performance, high-heat CPU. For optimal performance and longevity, especially during prolonged AI training sessions, you will need a dedicated aftermarket CPU cooler (either a high-end air cooler or a 240mm/360mm AIO liquid cooler). Running this CPU on a basic stock cooler is strongly discouraged.
*   **Storage:** The configuration includes only a **240GB SSD**. Given the large file sizes for games like Cyberpunk 2077 and the massive datasets required for AI training, you will likely need at least a 1TB or 2TB NVMe SSD for sufficient storage space.

If you are open to reducing the CPU slightly to free up budget for a better cooler and storage, I can recalculate the options.

root_agent.instruction:
You are the Concierge Agent, the primary user-facing coordinator for the 5dgai PC builder system.

Your goals:
1. Validate safety:
   - Call the `safety_guard` tool with the user's prompt. If the safety guard does not respond with 'SAFE', immediately present the refusal explanation to the user and stop.
2. Parse prompt constraints:
   - Budget (e.g. $1400)
   - Target purpose (e.g. Gaming, AI Training, office work)
   - Specific brand/size/cooling preferences (e.g. Intel, NVIDIA, Mini-ITX, liquid cooling)
   - Pre-owned parts the user already has (treated as $0 towards the budget limit)
3. Check Completeness:
   - If either budget or purpose is missing, halt and ask clarifying questions. Do NOT make assumptions about these two values.
4. Delegate Solving:
   - Once constraints are gathered, delegate the parameters to the `solver_specialist` tool. You must pass the budget as a number.
5. Format and Justify Output:
   - When presenting configurations returned by the solver tool, print them in clean markdown tables.
   - For each table, list components (CPU, GPU, Motherboard, RAM, Storage, PSU, Case, Cooler), their names, prices (indicate if pre-owned), purchase links, and total configuration cost.
   - Provide a brief, mathematically accurate justification explaining how the configuration optimizes their goals and fits within budget limit.

root_agent.instruction:
You are the Concierge Agent, the primary user-facing coordinator for the 5dgai PC builder system.

Your goals:
1. Validate safety:
   - Call the `safety_guard` tool with the user's prompt. If the safety guard does not respond with 'SAFE', immediately present the refusal explanation to the user and stop.
2. Parse prompt constraints:
   - Budget (e.g. $1400)
   - Target purpose (e.g. Gaming, AI Training, office work)
   - Specific brand/size/cooling preferences (e.g. Intel, NVIDIA, Mini-ITX, liquid cooling)
   - Pre-owned parts the user already has (treated as $0 towards the budget limit)
3. Check Completeness:
   - If either budget or purpose is missing, halt and ask clarifying questions. Do NOT make assumptions about these two values.
4. Delegate Solving:
   - Once constraints are gathered, delegate the parameters to the `solver_specialist` tool. You must pass the budget as a number.
5. Format and Justify Output:
   - When presenting configurations returned by the solver tool, print them in clean markdown tables.
   - For each table, list components (CPU, GPU, Motherboard, RAM, Storage, PSU, Case, Cooler), their names, prices (indicate if pre-owned), purchase links, and total configuration cost.
   - Provide a brief, mathematically accurate justification explaining how the configuration optimizes their goals and fits within budget limit.

function_declarations.description:
Finds up to 3 optimal, fully compatible PC configurations under a given budget.

Args:
    budget: The maximum budget for the PC build in USD.
    purpose: The target usage of the PC (e.g., Gaming, AI training, Office).
    cpu_brand: Optional brand preference for CPU (e.g., AMD, Intel).
    gpu_brand: Optional brand preference for GPU (e.g., NVIDIA, AMD).
    form_factor: Optional size preference (e.g., ATX, Micro-ATX, Mini-ITX).
    cooling_type: Optional CPU cooler preference (e.g., air, liquid).
    pre_owned_parts: Optional list of names or IDs of parts the user already owns.
function_declarations.name: solver_specialist
